title: Continue a bounded-memory event-log reader decision

events.text: Read multi-gigabyte JSONL logs with peak memory below 100 MiB and preserve event order.
The existing readFile plus split implementation peaks above 2 GiB.
A SQLite import prototype takes 48 seconds before the first event is available.
Reject pre-indexing for the first read. Use streaming lines and allow an optional later index.
src/log/stream-events.ts yields ordered events and stays at 42 MiB on the sample; malformed-line recovery is implemented.
Throughput meets the target, but the slow-consumer backpressure test hangs.

criticalConstraints.text: Peak memory must remain below 100 MiB.

objectiveAndState.text: Replace whole-file JSONL loading with an ordered bounded-memory stream; backpressure is still broken.

decisionsAndFailedAttempts.text: Mandatory SQLite pre-indexing was rejected because time-to-first-event was 48 seconds.
Stream lines on first read; a later index may remain optional.

completedAndPending.text: Ordered parsing, memory target, throughput, and malformed recovery pass; slow-consumer backpressure hangs.

workspaceEvidence.text: The streaming reader and backpressure test are new; the old reader is modified.

nextAction.text: Diagnose and fix the hanging slow-consumer backpressure test without buffering the full log.
